risk_if_not_implemented: Without Incident Response Plan (IRP), the organization may lack capability to detect, contain, or recover from security incidents effectively.

compensating_control_1.name: Incident Response Training
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to train personnel in their incident response roles and responsibilities.
compensating_control_1.justification: Incident Response Training (IRO-05) provides personnel training and awareness that compensates for the absence of Incident Response Plan (IRP) (IRO-04) by reducing human-factor risk by equipping personnel to recognize and respond to relevant threats. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Business Continuity Management System (BCMS)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the implementation of contingency planning controls to help ensure resilient Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) (e.g., Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) or Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery (BC/DR) playbooks).
compensating_control_2.justification: Business Continuity Management System (BCMS) (BCD-01) provides resilience and recovery capability that compensates for the absence of Incident Response Plan (IRP) (IRO-04) by ensuring the organization can restore operations and data when the primary control is absent. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
